_meta.title: Empty-Space Encounter Beats
_meta.description: In-flight belt encounters picked at module-load by the Phase 3a.1 travel system. When the captain transits a non-station hex (deep_belt, lane, anomaly), the travel system rolls one of these beats. Each resolves through crew choices; deltas feed the ledger and AI state.
_meta._credits: All beats are minimal by design — 1-2 sentences of prose, 2-3 choices. They resolve in a single screen, then return to travel. Longer narrative (station encounters, legacy traces) lives in separate manifests. These beats are the ambient texture of the belt: the things that happen between destinations.

distress_call_1.prose: A distress ping cuts through the comms — a NAC courier pinballed off her route, hull intact but navigation dead. She's burning battery on the signal. You have enough fuel to reach her, but the detour costs time.

choices.text: Ignore the ping. Stay on route.
Strip her beacon. Leave the hull.

distress_call_1_rescue.prose: You match velocity. The courier docks with a shudder and a hiss. She's young, shaking, grateful in that clipped NAC way that doesn't say thank you. Her cargo manifest shows medical supplies — bound for a RRA outpost you passed two ticks ago.

choices.text: Take her to the RRA outpost. It's a delay.
Drop her at the next lane junction. Move on.

distress_call_1_ignore.prose: You cut the comms. The ping loops for six more seconds and then stops. {crew_name_one} doesn't look at you. The ship hums. The belt doesn't care.

choices.text: Log the ping as noise. Move on.
Have {crew_name_one} log it. You don't sign.

distress_call_1_strip.prose: You take the beacon — still warm, still pinging — and leave the courier to the belt. The beacon's log shows she was running for an ED subsidiary, off-books. Someone paid her to be here. Someone paid her to be ignored.

choices.text: Keep the beacon. Sell it at Coral.

passing_stranger_1.prose: A ship drifts past — ED registry, old hull, running dark. No hail. It matches your vector for thirty seconds, then peels away. Someone on that bridge saw you and chose not to speak.

passing_stranger_1_hail.prose: The channel opens. A woman's voice — tired, not hostile. "You're the new {genship_id} captain. I ran your predecessor's run. They didn't make it past Coral." The channel cuts before you can respond.

passing_stranger_1_track.prose: You burn a tick of fuel and close the distance. The ED ship docks at a derelict station — uncharted, not on your map. You mark the coordinates. Someone will want this.

ship_failure_1.prose: The port nacelle coughs. Not a sound you want to hear in open belt. The drive stutters, catches, stutters again. {crew_name_two} is already in the engine room before you call her name.

choices.text: Reroute through the starboard. Limp forward.
Kill the drive. Drift and repair.

ship_failure_1_diagnose.prose: The diagnostic reads: fuel line contamination. Something in the last refuel was wrong — water, or worse, a tracing agent. {crew_name_two} bleeds the line. The drive catches. You're running clean again, but someone at that outpost knew what they were pumping.

choices.text: Log the contamination. Someone did this.
Keep it quiet. It was an accident.

ship_failure_1_reroute.prose: Starboard holds. You limp forward on half thrust. It costs fuel — the belt doesn't forgive inefficiency. {crew_name_two} watches the port nacelle from the engine room camera. She doesn't say what she's thinking.

choices.text: Push through. Make the station on time.

ship_failure_1_drift.prose: You kill the drive. The ship goes silent — no hum, no vibration, just the sound of your own breathing. {crew_name_two} works in the open engine bay with a hand-lamp and a wrench. It takes an hour. She comes back grease-streaked and calm. "It's done."

crew_fight_1.prose: You hear it before you see it — {crew_name_one}'s voice, sharp and low, cutting through the galley wall. {crew_name_two} answers with something louder. It's about rations. It's never about rations.

choices.text: Walk away. Let them sort it.
Round of brandy. Both of them.

crew_fight_1_mediate.prose: You step into the galley. Both of them stop — not because you outrank them, but because you're there. {crew_name_one} sits. {crew_name_two} pours water. You ask what's actually wrong. It takes ten minutes. It's about the run. It's always about the run.

choices.text: Table it. One problem at a time.

crew_fight_1_side.prose: You back {crew_name_one}. {crew_name_two} goes quiet — not agreement, just containment. She leaves the galley. The ship feels smaller. {crew_name_one} nods at you. It's not gratitude. It's confirmation.

crew_fight_1_walk.prose: You leave. They fight. It ends — not because it's resolved, but because they're tired. {crew_name_one} doesn't speak to {crew_name_two} for two ticks. The ship runs. The ledger doesn't track this. But you know.

choices.text: Log it privately. For the ledger.

crew_fight_1_brandy.prose: You pull the bottle — the good stuff, saved for a moment that isn't this. But you pour anyway. Three glasses. {crew_name_one} laughs first. {crew_name_two} doesn't. By the end they're talking about the run again, not the rations. Not fixed. But bridged.
